title: Athyrium hachijoense
taxonomy: Athyrium hachijoense is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Athyrium, which comprises several species of ferns commonly known as lady ferns. The specific epithet "hachijoense" refers to its native habitat, which is the Hachijojima island in Japan. This fern is native to East Asia and is known for its unique and distinctive frond structure. As a member of the Athyrium genus, Athyrium hachijoense is closely related to other lady fern species found in the region.

intro: Athyrium hachijoense, also known as Hachijo fern, is a species of fern endemic to Japan. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress.
